params.description: Horizontal data table appears. Marbles in Jars data. Jar A: 7, Jar B: 12, Jar C: 19, Jar D: 23.

beats.text: Here's data about marbles. Look at the biggest number: 23. That's pretty small!

elements.description: Horizontal data table showing Marbles in Jars data. Jar A: 7, Jar B: 12, Jar C: 19, Jar D: 23.

params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears with scale selector showing all four buttons. No scale selected yet. Marbles in Jars data.

beats.text: This graph is horizontal. The bars go sideways. Let's check which scales fit. Try clicking each scale.
Click each scale to see if 23 fits.

validator.description: Student clicked all four scale buttons to explore whether 23 fits on each scale. All scales fit.

beats.text: All four scales fit! Let's look at Scale of 1.

elements.description: Horizontal data table showing Marbles in Jars data. Jar A: 7, Jar B: 12, Jar C: 19, Jar D: 23.
Horizontal bar graph with scale selector visible. All four scale options (1, 2, 5, 10) marked with checkmarks. No scale currently applied to graph. Marbles in Jars data.

beats.text: Click Scale of 1 to see what it looks like.

validator.description: Student clicked Scale of 1. Graph displays with scale of 1, showing 24 tick marks (0 through 23).

params.description: All 24 tick marks on the vertical axis highlight to emphasize the density.

beats.text: It works. Every number lands exactly. But look at all those lines on the side!

elements.description: Horizontal data table showing Marbles in Jars data. Jar A: 7, Jar B: 12, Jar C: 19, Jar D: 23.
Horizontal bar graph set to scale of 1. Axis displays 24 tick marks (0 through 23), all highlighted. All four bars visible and fit within boundary. Marbles in Jars data.
